preparation_method: served with cream sauce and jus

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Ravioli del Plin is approximately 280 g, comprising around 10–14 individual pinched pasta parcels with sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pasta, fresh-refrigerated, plain, as purchased (FDC ID 168930) and Ravioli, meat-filled, fresh (FDC ID 168931); adapted with cream sauce and veal jus component estimation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for Ravioli del Plin. Values are adapted from USDA FDC entries for fresh meat-filled ravioli (FDC ID 168931) as the primary anchor, adjusted upward for fat and energy to account for the Parmesan cream sauce and veal jus components typical of this dish as served at Ricciotti Singapore. Protein and carbohydrate values are anchored to the fresh pasta and beef filling data. Sodium is elevated to reflect the cream, Parmesan, and jus components. All values represent a composite estimate per 100 g of the plated dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fresh pasta / filled pasta analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ravioli del Plin. GI of 40 is estimated from the closest analogous food: fresh egg pasta / filled pasta entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021), which report GI values of 35–46 for fresh egg-based pasta and filled pasta products. A midpoint of 40 is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 58.8) / 100 ≈ 23.5, where carbohydrate per 280 g serving = 21.0 × 2.8 = 58.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from Parmesan cheese, veal jus, and cream sauce
hypertension.guidance: A 280 g serving provides approximately 1092 mg sodium (~47% of the 2300 mg daily limit), so individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced-salt preparation, and avoid adding table salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined pasta carbohydrates combined with cream-based sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 23.5 per restaurant serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion, pair with a non-starchy vegetable side, and monitor postprandial glucose response, as the cream and fat content may slow but not eliminate the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from Parmesan cream sauce and beef filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A full restaurant serving delivers approximately 11.8 g saturated fat (~59% of the 20 g daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association), so individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption and choose smaller portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ravioli del Plin is a traditional handmade stuffed pasta originating from the Piedmont (Piemonte) region of northern Italy. The name 'plin' derives from the Piedmontese dialect word for 'pinch', referring to the characteristic technique of pinching the pasta dough closed to form small, tightly sealed parcels. Each piece is typically about 3–4 cm long. The filling classically contains braised or roasted meat — in this restaurant version, seasoned beef — combined with Parmesan cheese, eggs, and aromatics such as nutmeg. The pasta is made from fresh egg dough, giving it a tender, silky texture that contrasts with the rich, savoury filling. At Ricciotti Singapore, the dish is served with a Parmesan cream sauce, sautéed porcini mushrooms, and a veal jus reduction, creating a layered umami profile with earthy, creamy, and meaty notes. Nutritionally, a restaurant portion (~280 g) provides approximately 546 kcal, 27 g protein, 24 g fat, and 59 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is elevated (~1092 mg per serving) due to the Parmesan, jus, and cream components, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 40 (low), consistent with fresh egg pasta, though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at ~23.5 due to the portion size. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies. It is a culturally significant dish of Piedmontese cucina povera tradition, now elevated in fine-dining contexts.

ingredient_synonyms.veal jus: fondo di vitello, veal stock reduction, jus de veau
ingredient_synonyms.porcini mushrooms: Boletus edulis, ceps, porcini secchi (dried porcini)
ingredient_synonyms.fresh egg pasta: pasta fresca all'uovo, fresh pasta dough
ingredient_synonyms.beef filling: carne di manzo, braised beef stuffing

confidence_reason: No exact match for Ravioli del Plin exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB, USDA, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC fresh meat-filled ravioli entries with component-based adjustment for the cream sauce and veal jus. GI is estimated from analogous fresh egg pasta entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this adaptation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this adaptation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this adaptation. All -1 values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source, not a zero value. No core macro is zero; all four core macros are positive and nutritionally plausible for a meat-filled pasta dish with cream sauce. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate from fresh egg pasta / filled pasta data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr); gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule. Glycemic load per serving = (40 × 58.8) / 100 = 23.5 g, where 58.8 g carbohydrate is derived from 21.0 g/100g × 280 g serving. This record covers the Ricciotti Singapore restaurant version; home-prepared versions may differ in fat and sodium. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input is not applicable to this dish and has been discarded.
